'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} * {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家}}
== External Links == * [http://www.natasho.co.jp/koyomi/rekisi/index.html History of the Natasho Calendar]

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.

=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.

He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.

Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.

=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} * {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家}}
== External Links == * [http://www.natasho.co.jp/koyomi/rekisi/index.html History of the Natasho Calendar]

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} * {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家}}
== External Links == * [http://www.natasho.co.jp/koyomi/rekisi/index.html History of the Natasho Calendar]

[[sn:Tsuchimikado Hisanaga]][[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:土御門久脩]]

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560年}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625年2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560th year }}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} is believed to have ended.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}} * {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家}}
== External Links == * [http://www.natasho.co.jp/koyomi/rekisi/index.html History of the Natasho Calendar]

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft}} - {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|1560|ja|1560th year|12=draft}}) | death_date = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 2, {{ill|January 18 (old calendar)|ja|1月18日|12=draft}} ({{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=draft}}) | titles = {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}}, {{ill|Counselor who does not participate|ja|非参議|12=draft}}, {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakyo no Daibu|ja|左衛門佐|12=draft}} | served = {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|ja|正親町天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Go-Mizunoo|ja|後水尾天皇|12=draft}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=draft}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩|12=draft}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=draft}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasunaga|ja|泰重|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kurahashi Yasuyoshi|ja|倉橋泰吉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲|12=draft}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|12=draft}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=draft}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}. Hisanaga attained the rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}} and held positions such as {{ill|Non-councilors|ja|非参議|lt=Non-councilor|12=draft}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|12=draft}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=draft}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=draft}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|12=draft}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=draft}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|12=draft}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|12=draft}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=draft}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft}} is believed to have ended.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 土御門久脩 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[安土桃山時代]] - [[江戸時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[永禄]]3年（[[1560年]]） | 死没 = [[寛永]]2年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1625年]][[2月24日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 勘解由小路在綱 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従三位]]・[[非参議]]、[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]、[[衛門府|左衛門佐]] | 主君 = [[正親町天皇]]→[[後陽成天皇]]→[[後水尾天皇]]
[[織田信長]]→[[豊臣秀吉]]→[[徳川家康]]→[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]]→[[徳川家光|家光]] | 氏族 = [[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]][[嫡流]][[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]] | 父母 = 父：[[土御門有脩]]　母：不詳 | 兄弟 = '''久脩'''、[[勧修寺晴豊]]室 | 妻 = [[三淵晴員]]娘 | 子 = '''[[土御門泰重|泰重]]'''、[[倉橋泰吉]]、[[五辻斉仲]]など | 特記事項 = }} '''土御門 久脩'''（つちみかど ひさなが）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期にかけての[[公卿]]・[[陰陽師]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]・[[土御門有脩]]{{efn2|永禄5年（1563年）に正式に土御門を称する。}}の子。[[官位]]は[[従三位]]・[[非参議]]、[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]、[[衛門府|左衛門佐]]。[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]][[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]31代当主。 == 経歴 == 幼き時より[[天文道]]や[[暦道]]を学ぶ。[[元亀]]3年（[[1572年]]）に元服し、天正元年（[[1573年]]）[[12月28日 (旧暦)|12月28日]]・14歳の時に、朝廷[[陰陽寮]]の長官・陰陽頭となる。 当時、土御門家と並んで[[陰陽道宗家]]の双璧であった[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]][[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|勘解由小路家]]の当主・[[勘解由小路在富|在富]]の唯一の息子である[[賀茂在昌]]は、[[永禄]]7年（[[1564年]]）に妻子を連れて京を出奔して、[[キリシタン]]となって[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]に留学していた。在富は後継者が不在のまま、永禄8年（[[1565年]]）死去。嗣子がないため、久脩の祖父・[[土御門有春]]の四男で久脩の叔父に当たる福寿丸（1553-1575・13歳）を[[勘解由小路在高]]として養子に入れて相続していた[[#tajima|但馬荒人]] - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b4%aa%b2%f2%cd%b3%be%ae%cf%a9%ba%df%b9%e2 勘解由小路在高]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}が、在高は[[天正]]3年（[[1575年]]）に23歳で夭折してしまう。 これを受けて同年、16歳で久脩は勘解由小路在綱（かでのこうじ あきつな）と改姓改名して、勘解由小路家を嗣ぐことになった。同年、[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[馬寮|左馬助]]に叙任される。 しかしそれも束の間、天正5年（[[1577年]]）[[1月2日 (旧暦)|1月2日]]、父・土御門有脩が死亡してしまう。これに伴い、唯一の嫡子である久脩改め在綱は、同年[[3月26日 (旧暦)|3月26日]]、土御門久脩へと復姓復名し、土御門家当主を嗣いだ[[#tajima|但馬荒人]] - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b2%ec%cc%d0%ba%df%be%bb 賀茂在昌]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}。 その後、[[織田信長]]、[[豊臣秀吉]]と仕え、天正8年（[[1580年]]）には21歳で[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[天文博士]]に叙任された[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%80%89%e6%a9%8b%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#hisanaga625 土御門家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}が、天文博士は陰陽頭の格下であり、[[位階]]こそ昇格しているものの、[[官職]]の上では降格である。また、その後は長らく官位昇進が無かった。 天正10年（[[1582年]]）、織田信長の推挙により「公家成り」を果たし、以後、土御門家は地下から堂上公家となる（江戸時代に「半家」とされた堂上公家）。同年、次の閏月を天正11年（[[1583年]]）閏1月とするべきだとする陰陽寮が作成した京暦と、それとは異なる天正10年（1582年）閏12月とするべきだという[[伊豆国]]の[[三嶋大社]]が作成した[[三島暦]]とで[[改暦]]問題が発生した。久脩は信長に呼び出されて[[安土城]]に向かい、論争をしたが決着は付かなかった。しかし、信長は三島暦にするように朝廷に働きかけを行っている。公家衆は無論京暦を支持したのであるが、信長は6月1日に上洛すると再びこの話を持ち出している。ところが、翌日に[[本能寺の変]]が発生して信長が自刃したためにこの話は立ち消えとなった（[[改暦#天正10年の例]]参照）。 久脩は[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に秀吉の怒りを買ってしまい、多数の陰陽師とともに[[尾張国]]に[[流罪|配流]]された[[#kiba1985|木場明志 1985年（『陰陽道叢書２中世』）]] p.180-181。これは[[豊臣秀次]]の事件に連座したものといわれている[[#saito2014|斎藤英喜 2014年]] p.125-128。 [[慶長]]5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]ののちの11月[[#tajima|但馬荒人]] - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b4%aa%b2%f2%cd%b3%be%ae%cf%a9%ba%df%bf%ae%a1%a7%c2%e8%c6%f3%a4%ce%c2%a9%bb%d2 勘解由小路在信：第二の息子]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}、[[勅命]]により再び[[上洛]]。翌年には代々の所領で別邸のある[[若狭国]][[名田庄村|名田庄]]を引き上げ、京郊外の[[梅小路通|梅小路]]村（[https://kyotofukoh.jp/report882.html 梅林寺]・[https://kyotofukoh.jp/report883.html 稲住神社]・[https://kyotofukoh.jp/report881.html 円光寺]付近）に邸宅を構えた。[[徳川家康]]に[[陰陽道宗家]]と認められ、183石6斗の[[家禄]]を与えられた。また、公家[[昵懇衆]]として家康に仕えた。 徳川幕府では家康、[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]]、[[徳川家光|家光]]と徳川家三代の将軍宣下に伴う、また[[後陽成天皇]]、[[後水尾天皇]]の即位に伴う[[六道冥官祭|天曹地府祭]]を執行している（後陽成天皇の天曹地府祭は即位後15年を経た慶長6年（[[1601年]]）[[1月30日]]に斎行{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tatara.net/ETC/eki/onmyo.html|title=宗教としての陰陽道─陰陽道の即位儀礼、天曹地府祭|author=黒岩重人|accessdate=2019-10-04}}）。 慶長16年（[[1611年]]）、15年ぶりに官位昇進して[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[衛門府|左衛門権佐]]に叙任され、慶長18年（[[1613年]]）には[[従四位|従四位上]]・[[衛門府|左衛門佐]]、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）には[[正四位|正四位下]]に叙任されている。 元和5年（[[1619年]]）9月、2代将軍・[[徳川秀忠]]の娘である[[徳川和子|和子]]入内を目前にして発覚した宮中内の不祥事（特に[[およつ御寮人事件]]）に伴い、｢[[禁中並公家諸法度]]｣違反とされ、[[京都所司代]]・[[板倉勝重]]を通して幕府より内裏への出仕停止・謹慎を申し渡される{{efn2|幕府によれば宮中に女[[歌舞伎]]や[[遊女]]を呼んで昼間から酒宴を開き、山椒茶をすすり、[[ミョウガ|茗荷]]を食して再び酒宴に明け暮れ、[[囲碁]]・[[かるた|歌留多]]・[[賭博]]に興じた等々の罪状が挙げられた。}}。 後水尾天皇は一連の処分に激しく不満を示し、弟の[[近衛信尋]]への譲位までほのめかした。これを諫めるため、公家側は元関白･[[近衛前久]]や現職の[[関白]]・[[九条幸家|九条忠栄]]が{{efn2|忠栄の妻･完子([[豊臣完子]])は徳川秀忠夫人･於江([[崇源院]])と夫人の前夫･[[豊臣秀勝]]との子であり、和子の異父姉となる。}}、幕府側は和子入内における朝廷との折衝役であった[[藤堂高虎]]が仲介・奔走。結局、幕府に不満を持っていた[[勧修寺晴子|新上東門院]]崩御に伴って、和子入内に積極的であった天皇生母[[近衛前子|中和門院]]（中和門院は九条家出身）が台頭したこと、朝廷から和子入内の確約を幕府に示すことで久脩らは赦免・再出仕となる{{efn2|『泰重卿記』によれば、久脩らは流罪にもなりかねない状況であったが、関白･九条忠栄の弁明、さらに自らも出仕停止処分を受けていた堀河康胤が久脩を庇った結果、出仕停止で済んだと記録されている。}}。 元和7年（[[1621年]]）に[[従三位]]・[[非参議]]に叙せられ、[[公卿]]に列格している。 == 系譜 == === 家族 === *父：[[土御門有脩]] *母：不詳 *妻：[[三淵晴員]]の娘 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[土御門泰重]] - [[兵衛府|左兵衛督]]、[[従二位]]・[[非参議]] **男子：[[倉橋泰吉]] - [[倉橋家]]初代、[[民部省|民部卿]] **男子：[[五辻斉仲]] - [[神楽]]を司る[[五辻家]]（[[宇多源氏]]末裔の公家）、[[非参議]]・[[五辻之仲]]の養子 === 末裔 === 土御門久脩を経て伝わってきた[[安倍晴明]]の男系血脈は、[[宇多源氏]][[綾小路家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E7%B6%BE%E5%B0%8F%E8%B7%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E7%BE%BD%E6%9E%97%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#tosimune770 綾小路家（羽林家）－綾小路俊宗]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|男系を辿ると[[藤原北家]][[閑院流]][[三条家]]に行き至る。
[[藤原師輔]]-[[藤原公季|（閑院）公季]]-4代-[[三条実行]]-2代-[[正親町三条公氏]]-11代-滋野井季国-五辻為仲-之仲-綾小路高有-3代-倉橋有儀-土御門泰栄}}の子で[[倉橋家]]（土御門久脩の末裔・安倍氏支流）の養子となった[[倉橋有儀]]（[[1738年]] - [[1784年]]）[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%80%89%e6%a9%8b%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#yasuyuki858 倉橋家（半家）－倉橋有儀]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}と、その息子で土御門家の養子となった[[土御門泰栄]]（[[1758年]] - [[1806年]]）[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%9C%9F%E5%BE%A1%E9%96%80%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuek806 土御門家（半家）－土御門泰栄]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}の代で断絶しており、現在の土御門家[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%9C%9F%E5%BE%A1%E9%96%80%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuek806 土御門家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}・倉橋家[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%80%89%e6%a9%8b%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 倉橋家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}当主はいずれも更に養子相続を繰り返した結果、久脩の父・[[土御門有脩|有脩]]から数えて共に4回も女系を経た遠縁となってしまっている[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%9C%9F%E5%BE%A1%E9%96%80%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuek806 土御門家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|よって、「安倍晴明の男系直系末裔」は、信憑性のある公的な系譜上、現存しない。}}。 一方、久脩の血筋は、5代目の男系子孫・[[倉橋泰章]]（[[1687年]] - [[1753年]]）[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%80%89%E6%A9%8B%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuaki753 倉橋家（半家）－倉橋泰章]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}の娘が[[萩原員領]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E8%90%A9%E5%8E%9F%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#kazt784 萩原家（半家）－萩原員領]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}の妻になり子を残しているため、[[卜部氏]][[萩原家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E8%90%A9%E5%8E%9F%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 萩原家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[萩原家]]：土御門久脩-倉橋泰吉-泰房-泰貞-泰章（母は[[土御門泰福]]の娘）-萩原員領室-員幹-員維-員光-員種-員振-兼武（-兼昭）}}{{efn2|男系を辿ると[[藤原北家]]日野流（真夏流）に行き至る。
[[藤原真夏]]-12代-[[日野資実]]-3代-[[柳原資明]]-1代-[[町資藤]]-2代-広橋守光-3代-油小路隆基-1代-萩原兼澄-1代-員領}}とそこから養子に出た幾つかの[[堂上家|堂上公家]]・[[華族]]の子孫に受け継がれている。これに由来し、その後も男系相続された（女系を1回のみしか経ていない）末裔に限っても、[[岩倉具視]]とその玄孫の[[京都大学]]教授[[岩倉具忠]]ら[[岩倉家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%B2%A9%E5%80%89%E6%B0%8F%EF%BC%88%E7%BE%BD%E6%9E%97%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 岩倉氏（羽林家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[岩倉家]]：萩原員領-員幹-[[堀河康親]]-[[岩倉具視]]-[[岩倉具定|具定]]-[[岩倉具張|具張]]-[[岩倉具忠|具忠]]}}{{efn2|岩倉具視の子孫としては、芸能人の[[小桜葉子]]（池端具子）、[[加山雄三]]（池端直亮）も久脩の血筋を受け継いでいる。（岩倉具視-具定-具顕-池端具子-直亮）}}、[[春日大社]]宮司花山院弘匡ら[[花山院家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E8%8A%B1%E5%B1%B1%E9%99%A2%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 花山院家（清華家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[花山院家]]：萩原員領-員幹-堀河康親-康隆-[[花山院親家]]-親忠-弘匡}}、[[太秦家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%A4%AA%E7%A7%A6%E5%AE%B6 太秦家]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[太秦家]]：萩原員領-員幹-堀河康親-康隆-親賀-太秦供親-[[太秦供康|供康]]-康光-康紀（-康志）}}などが現存する。 [[明治時代]]の土御門家当主・[[子爵]][[土御門晴榮]]も、萩原家から養子に出た[[卜部氏]][[錦織家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E9%8C%A6%E7%B9%94%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 錦織家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}からの養子のため、この系譜に連なる{{efn2|萩原員領-従言-錦織久雄-久隆-土御門晴榮}}。 {{see also|土御門有脩#末裔|土御門泰福#末裔}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist2}} === 出典 === {{reflist|refs= 但馬荒人 - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b4%aa%b2%f2%cd%b3%be%ae%cf%a9%ba%df%b9%cb 勘解由小路在綱]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}} }} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=木場明志|title=暦道賀茂家断絶の事―永禄～文禄期 宮廷陰陽道の動向|editor=北西弘先生還暦記念会編|publisher=吉川弘文館|journal=中世社会と一向一揆|year=1985}}（村山修一他編『陰陽道叢書２中世』、名著出版、1993年所載） * {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤英喜|title=陰陽師たちの日本史|publisher=角川学芸出版|series=角川選書546|year=2014}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/|author=ネケト|title=Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～|accessdate=2019-10-04}}{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}} == 関連項目 == *[[賀茂在昌]] *[[倉橋家]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.natasho.co.jp/koyomi/rekisi/index.html おおい町暦館HP] {{DEFAULTSORT:つちみかと ひさなか}} [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)|ひさなか]] [[Category:室町・安土桃山時代の公家]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:1560年生]] [[Category:1625年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[土御門久脩]] to [[Tsuchimikado Hisanaga]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 土御門久脩 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[安土桃山時代]] - [[江戸時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[永禄]]3年（[[1560年]]） | 死没 = [[寛永]]2年[[1月18日 (旧暦)|1月18日]]（[[1625年]][[2月24日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 勘解由小路在綱 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従三位]]・[[非参議]]、[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]、[[衛門府|左衛門佐]] | 主君 = [[正親町天皇]]→[[後陽成天皇]]→[[後水尾天皇]]
[[織田信長]]→[[豊臣秀吉]]→[[徳川家康]]→[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]]→[[徳川家光|家光]] | 氏族 = [[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]][[嫡流]][[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]] | 父母 = 父：[[土御門有脩]]　母：不詳 | 兄弟 = '''久脩'''、[[勧修寺晴豊]]室 | 妻 = [[三淵晴員]]娘 | 子 = '''[[土御門泰重|泰重]]'''、[[倉橋泰吉]]、[[五辻斉仲]]など | 特記事項 = }} '''土御門 久脩'''（つちみかど ひさなが）は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期にかけての[[公卿]]・[[陰陽師]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]・[[土御門有脩]]{{efn2|永禄5年（1563年）に正式に土御門を称する。}}の子。[[官位]]は[[従三位]]・[[非参議]]、[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]、[[衛門府|左衛門佐]]。[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]][[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]31代当主。 == 経歴 == 幼き時より[[天文道]]や[[暦道]]を学ぶ。[[元亀]]3年（[[1572年]]）に元服し、天正元年（[[1573年]]）[[12月28日 (旧暦)|12月28日]]・14歳の時に、朝廷[[陰陽寮]]の長官・陰陽頭となる。 当時、土御門家と並んで[[陰陽道宗家]]の双璧であった[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]][[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|勘解由小路家]]の当主・[[勘解由小路在富|在富]]の唯一の息子である[[賀茂在昌]]は、[[永禄]]7年（[[1564年]]）に妻子を連れて京を出奔して、[[キリシタン]]となって[[府内 (豊後国)|豊後府内]]に留学していた。在富は後継者が不在のまま、永禄8年（[[1565年]]）死去。嗣子がないため、久脩の祖父・[[土御門有春]]の四男で久脩の叔父に当たる福寿丸（1553-1575・13歳）を[[勘解由小路在高]]として養子に入れて相続していた[[#tajima|但馬荒人]] - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b4%aa%b2%f2%cd%b3%be%ae%cf%a9%ba%df%b9%e2 勘解由小路在高]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}が、在高は[[天正]]3年（[[1575年]]）に23歳で夭折してしまう。 これを受けて同年、16歳で久脩は勘解由小路在綱（かでのこうじ あきつな）と改姓改名して、勘解由小路家を嗣ぐことになった。同年、[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[馬寮|左馬助]]に叙任される。 しかしそれも束の間、天正5年（[[1577年]]）[[1月2日 (旧暦)|1月2日]]、父・土御門有脩が死亡してしまう。これに伴い、唯一の嫡子である久脩改め在綱は、同年[[3月26日 (旧暦)|3月26日]]、土御門久脩へと復姓復名し、土御門家当主を嗣いだ[[#tajima|但馬荒人]] - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b2%ec%cc%d0%ba%df%be%bb 賀茂在昌]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}。 その後、[[織田信長]]、[[豊臣秀吉]]と仕え、天正8年（[[1580年]]）には21歳で[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[天文博士]]に叙任された[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%80%89%e6%a9%8b%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#hisanaga625 土御門家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}が、天文博士は陰陽頭の格下であり、[[位階]]こそ昇格しているものの、[[官職]]の上では降格である。また、その後は長らく官位昇進が無かった。 天正10年（[[1582年]]）、織田信長の推挙により「公家成り」を果たし、以後、土御門家は地下から堂上公家となる（江戸時代に「半家」とされた堂上公家）。同年、次の閏月を天正11年（[[1583年]]）閏1月とするべきだとする陰陽寮が作成した京暦と、それとは異なる天正10年（1582年）閏12月とするべきだという[[伊豆国]]の[[三嶋大社]]が作成した[[三島暦]]とで[[改暦]]問題が発生した。久脩は信長に呼び出されて[[安土城]]に向かい、論争をしたが決着は付かなかった。しかし、信長は三島暦にするように朝廷に働きかけを行っている。公家衆は無論京暦を支持したのであるが、信長は6月1日に上洛すると再びこの話を持ち出している。ところが、翌日に[[本能寺の変]]が発生して信長が自刃したためにこの話は立ち消えとなった（[[改暦#天正10年の例]]参照）。 久脩は[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に秀吉の怒りを買ってしまい、多数の陰陽師とともに[[尾張国]]に[[流罪|配流]]された[[#kiba1985|木場明志 1985年（『陰陽道叢書２中世』）]] p.180-181。これは[[豊臣秀次]]の事件に連座したものといわれている[[#saito2014|斎藤英喜 2014年]] p.125-128。 [[慶長]]5年（[[1600年]]）[[関ヶ原の戦い]]ののちの11月[[#tajima|但馬荒人]] - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b4%aa%b2%f2%cd%b3%be%ae%cf%a9%ba%df%bf%ae%a1%a7%c2%e8%c6%f3%a4%ce%c2%a9%bb%d2 勘解由小路在信：第二の息子]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}、[[勅命]]により再び[[上洛]]。翌年には代々の所領で別邸のある[[若狭国]][[名田庄村|名田庄]]を引き上げ、京郊外の[[梅小路通|梅小路]]村（[https://kyotofukoh.jp/report882.html 梅林寺]・[https://kyotofukoh.jp/report883.html 稲住神社]・[https://kyotofukoh.jp/report881.html 円光寺]付近）に邸宅を構えた。[[徳川家康]]に[[陰陽道宗家]]と認められ、183石6斗の[[家禄]]を与えられた。また、公家[[昵懇衆]]として家康に仕えた。 徳川幕府では家康、[[徳川秀忠|秀忠]]、[[徳川家光|家光]]と徳川家三代の将軍宣下に伴う、また[[後陽成天皇]]、[[後水尾天皇]]の即位に伴う[[六道冥官祭|天曹地府祭]]を執行している（後陽成天皇の天曹地府祭は即位後15年を経た慶長6年（[[1601年]]）[[1月30日]]に斎行{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tatara.net/ETC/eki/onmyo.html|title=宗教としての陰陽道─陰陽道の即位儀礼、天曹地府祭|author=黒岩重人|accessdate=2019-10-04}}）。 慶長16年（[[1611年]]）、15年ぶりに官位昇進して[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[衛門府|左衛門権佐]]に叙任され、慶長18年（[[1613年]]）には[[従四位|従四位上]]・[[衛門府|左衛門佐]]、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）には[[正四位|正四位下]]に叙任されている。 元和5年（[[1619年]]）9月、2代将軍・[[徳川秀忠]]の娘である[[徳川和子|和子]]入内を目前にして発覚した宮中内の不祥事（特に[[およつ御寮人事件]]）に伴い、｢[[禁中並公家諸法度]]｣違反とされ、[[京都所司代]]・[[板倉勝重]]を通して幕府より内裏への出仕停止・謹慎を申し渡される{{efn2|幕府によれば宮中に女[[歌舞伎]]や[[遊女]]を呼んで昼間から酒宴を開き、山椒茶をすすり、[[ミョウガ|茗荷]]を食して再び酒宴に明け暮れ、[[囲碁]]・[[かるた|歌留多]]・[[賭博]]に興じた等々の罪状が挙げられた。}}。 後水尾天皇は一連の処分に激しく不満を示し、弟の[[近衛信尋]]への譲位までほのめかした。これを諫めるため、公家側は元関白･[[近衛前久]]や現職の[[関白]]・[[九条幸家|九条忠栄]]が{{efn2|忠栄の妻･完子([[豊臣完子]])は徳川秀忠夫人･於江([[崇源院]])と夫人の前夫･[[豊臣秀勝]]との子であり、和子の異父姉となる。}}、幕府側は和子入内における朝廷との折衝役であった[[藤堂高虎]]が仲介・奔走。結局、幕府に不満を持っていた[[勧修寺晴子|新上東門院]]崩御に伴って、和子入内に積極的であった天皇生母[[近衛前子|中和門院]]（中和門院は九条家出身）が台頭したこと、朝廷から和子入内の確約を幕府に示すことで久脩らは赦免・再出仕となる{{efn2|『泰重卿記』によれば、久脩らは流罪にもなりかねない状況であったが、関白･九条忠栄の弁明、さらに自らも出仕停止処分を受けていた堀河康胤が久脩を庇った結果、出仕停止で済んだと記録されている。}}。 元和7年（[[1621年]]）に[[従三位]]・[[非参議]]に叙せられ、[[公卿]]に列格している。 == 系譜 == === 家族 === *父：[[土御門有脩]] *母：不詳 *妻：[[三淵晴員]]の娘 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[土御門泰重]] - [[兵衛府|左兵衛督]]、[[従二位]]・[[非参議]] **男子：[[倉橋泰吉]] - [[倉橋家]]初代、[[民部省|民部卿]] **男子：[[五辻斉仲]] - [[神楽]]を司る[[五辻家]]（[[宇多源氏]]末裔の公家）、[[非参議]]・[[五辻之仲]]の養子 === 末裔 === 土御門久脩を経て伝わってきた[[安倍晴明]]の男系血脈は、[[宇多源氏]][[綾小路家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E7%B6%BE%E5%B0%8F%E8%B7%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E7%BE%BD%E6%9E%97%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#tosimune770 綾小路家（羽林家）－綾小路俊宗]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|男系を辿ると[[藤原北家]][[閑院流]][[三条家]]に行き至る。
[[藤原師輔]]-[[藤原公季|（閑院）公季]]-4代-[[三条実行]]-2代-[[正親町三条公氏]]-11代-滋野井季国-五辻為仲-之仲-綾小路高有-3代-倉橋有儀-土御門泰栄}}の子で[[倉橋家]]（土御門久脩の末裔・安倍氏支流）の養子となった[[倉橋有儀]]（[[1738年]] - [[1784年]]）[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%80%89%e6%a9%8b%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89#yasuyuki858 倉橋家（半家）－倉橋有儀]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}と、その息子で土御門家の養子となった[[土御門泰栄]]（[[1758年]] - [[1806年]]）[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%9C%9F%E5%BE%A1%E9%96%80%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuek806 土御門家（半家）－土御門泰栄]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}の代で断絶しており、現在の土御門家[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%9C%9F%E5%BE%A1%E9%96%80%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuek806 土御門家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}・倉橋家[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e5%80%89%e6%a9%8b%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 倉橋家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}当主はいずれも更に養子相続を繰り返した結果、久脩の父・[[土御門有脩|有脩]]から数えて共に4回も女系を経た遠縁となってしまっている[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%9C%9F%E5%BE%A1%E9%96%80%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuek806 土御門家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|よって、「安倍晴明の男系直系末裔」は、信憑性のある公的な系譜上、現存しない。}}。 一方、久脩の血筋は、5代目の男系子孫・[[倉橋泰章]]（[[1687年]] - [[1753年]]）[[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%80%89%E6%A9%8B%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#yasuaki753 倉橋家（半家）－倉橋泰章]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}の娘が[[萩原員領]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E8%90%A9%E5%8E%9F%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89#kazt784 萩原家（半家）－萩原員領]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}の妻になり子を残しているため、[[卜部氏]][[萩原家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E8%90%A9%E5%8E%9F%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 萩原家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[萩原家]]：土御門久脩-倉橋泰吉-泰房-泰貞-泰章（母は[[土御門泰福]]の娘）-萩原員領室-員幹-員維-員光-員種-員振-兼武（-兼昭）}}{{efn2|男系を辿ると[[藤原北家]]日野流（真夏流）に行き至る。
[[藤原真夏]]-12代-[[日野資実]]-3代-[[柳原資明]]-1代-[[町資藤]]-2代-広橋守光-3代-油小路隆基-1代-萩原兼澄-1代-員領}}とそこから養子に出た幾つかの[[堂上家|堂上公家]]・[[華族]]の子孫に受け継がれている。これに由来し、その後も男系相続された（女系を1回のみしか経ていない）末裔に限っても、[[岩倉具視]]とその玄孫の[[京都大学]]教授[[岩倉具忠]]ら[[岩倉家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%B2%A9%E5%80%89%E6%B0%8F%EF%BC%88%E7%BE%BD%E6%9E%97%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 岩倉氏（羽林家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[岩倉家]]：萩原員領-員幹-[[堀河康親]]-[[岩倉具視]]-[[岩倉具定|具定]]-[[岩倉具張|具張]]-[[岩倉具忠|具忠]]}}{{efn2|岩倉具視の子孫としては、芸能人の[[小桜葉子]]（池端具子）、[[加山雄三]]（池端直亮）も久脩の血筋を受け継いでいる。（岩倉具視-具定-具顕-池端具子-直亮）}}、[[春日大社]]宮司花山院弘匡ら[[花山院家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E8%8A%B1%E5%B1%B1%E9%99%A2%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E6%B8%85%E8%8F%AF%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 花山院家（清華家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[花山院家]]：萩原員領-員幹-堀河康親-康隆-[[花山院親家]]-親忠-弘匡}}、[[太秦家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E5%A4%AA%E7%A7%A6%E5%AE%B6 太秦家]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}{{efn2|[[太秦家]]：萩原員領-員幹-堀河康親-康隆-親賀-太秦供親-[[太秦供康|供康]]-康光-康紀（-康志）}}などが現存する。 [[明治時代]]の土御門家当主・[[子爵]][[土御門晴榮]]も、萩原家から養子に出た[[卜部氏]][[錦織家]][[#reichs|Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～]] [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%AE%B6%E7%B3%BB%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88/%E9%8C%A6%E7%B9%94%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%88%E5%8D%8A%E5%AE%B6%EF%BC%89 錦織家（半家）]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}}からの養子のため、この系譜に連なる{{efn2|萩原員領-従言-錦織久雄-久隆-土御門晴榮}}。 {{see also|土御門有脩#末裔|土御門泰福#末裔}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist2}} === 出典 === {{reflist|refs= 但馬荒人 - [https://seesaawiki.jp/consume_mind/d/%b4%aa%b2%f2%cd%b3%be%ae%cf%a9%ba%df%b9%cb 勘解由小路在綱]{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}} }} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=木場明志|title=暦道賀茂家断絶の事―永禄～文禄期 宮廷陰陽道の動向|editor=北西弘先生還暦記念会編|publisher=吉川弘文館|journal=中世社会と一向一揆|year=1985}}（村山修一他編『陰陽道叢書２中世』、名著出版、1993年所載） * {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤英喜|title=陰陽師たちの日本史|publisher=角川学芸出版|series=角川選書546|year=2014}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://reichsarchiv.jp/|author=ネケト|title=Reichsarchiv ～世界帝王事典～|accessdate=2019-10-04}}{{信頼性要検証|date=2022-1}} == 関連項目 == *[[賀茂在昌]] *[[倉橋家]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.natasho.co.jp/koyomi/rekisi/index.html おおい町暦館HP] {{DEFAULTSORT:つちみかと ひさなか}} [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)|ひさなか]] [[Category:室町・安土桃山時代の公家]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:1560年生]] [[Category:1625年没]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土御門久脩]] {{Infobox noble | name = Tsuchimikado Hisanaga | image = | caption = | era = [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] - [[:en:Edo period|Edo '''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Biography ==

=== Early Life ===
Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family ===
Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.

=== Service and Achievements ===
Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.

In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.

=== Later Life ===
He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.

Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Family and Descendants ==
Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.
