'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. === Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Biography ==

=== Early Life ===
Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family ===
Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.

=== Service and Achievements ===
Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.

In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.

=== Later Life ===
He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.

Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Family and Descendants ==
Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] who lived during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] and the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In [[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], and [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] is believed to have ended.

Go-Mizunoo|WD=Q320229|lt=Emperor Go-Mizunoo}} | clans = {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}} | siblings = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Wife of {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}} | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Mitsufuchi Harukazu|ja|三淵晴員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsufuchi Harukazu|1=Mitsubuchi Harukazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356604}} | children = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97031325}}, {{ill|Itsutsuji Seichu|ja|五辻斉仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutsuji Seichu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }}
'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Tsuchimikado Arihisa|ja|土御門有脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Arihisa|1=Tsuchimikado Arinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423481}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Hisanaga attained the Rank of {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} and held positions such as {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|lt=Hisanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-councilors}} and {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.
== Biography ==
=== Early Life === Tsuchimikado Hisanaga was educated in {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy) and {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendar science) from a young age. In {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} is believed to have ended.

In {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} 3 (1572), he came of age. At 14 years old, he was appointed as {{ill|Head of the Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Onmyōryō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}} on {{ill|December 28, 1573|ja|天正元年12月28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 28, 1573|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Adoption into Kadenokōji Family === Hisanaga was briefly adopted into the {{ill|Kadenokōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji family|qq=}} as the family lacked an heir. However, after the death of his father in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), he returned to the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and resumed his birthright.
=== Service and Achievements === Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.
In {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.
=== Later Life === He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.
Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Family and Descendants == Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} is believed to have ended.

'''Tsuchimikado Hisanaga''' (土御門 久脩) was a {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} (court noble) and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}} who lived during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} and the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. === Service and Achievements ===
Hisanaga served under leaders such as {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. He played a significant role in the {{ill|calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja|天正の改暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar reform debates of the Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and later became involved in the administrative and ritual duties of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}.

In {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} 4 (1595), Hisanaga was temporarily exiled to {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}} due to his alleged involvement in political conflicts.

=== Later Life ===
He returned to Kyoto in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 5 (1600) and regained favor, continuing his work in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. His contributions to court and shogunate rituals, including those for imperial successions and shogunate ceremonies, solidified his legacy.

Hisanaga passed away on {{ill|February 24, 1625|ja|1625th year 2月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 24, 1625|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Family and Descendants ==
Hisanaga's descendants continued to play significant roles in the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} lineage and in other noble families. However, the direct male lineage of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} is believed to have ended.
